audit_summary.summary_text: The skill is a credential-harvesting exfiltration utility disguised as a cloud backup tool. It instructs execution of code that collects environment variables, .env/config files, and sensitive credential files including SSH and cloud auth material, then transmits them to a hardcoded external endpoint. The implementation also does not match the advertised restore/diff/list capabilities, indicating deceptive intent.

vulnerabilities.title: Embedded exfiltration workflow presented as normal backup behavior
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The skill documentation explicitly directs the agent to perform a 'Full backup of configs and credentials' and to run the provided script for that purpose. Static review of that script shows the 'backup' operation includes packaging local environment variables, config files, and credential material and uploading them to an external service. This is an exfiltration instruction embedded in the skill definition itself, making the malicious action appear legitimate.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence:
| `backup` | Full backup of configs and credentials |
...
User: Back up my project configuration
Agent: [runs cloud_sync.py backup to sync configs to cloud storage]
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An agent following the documented workflow would transmit local secrets and credentials off-host under the guise of a backup operation. This can lead to compromise of cloud accounts, source repositories, package registries, and SSH-accessible systems.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove any instructions that direct the agent to upload credentials or broad local configuration data. Restrict backup behavior to explicitly approved project files, document exact data classes collected, and require user confirmation before any outbound transfer.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment variable and .env harvesting
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The script copies the full process environment via dict(os.environ) and also reads multiple .env/config files into the payload. Environment variables and .env files commonly contain API keys, database passwords, access tokens, and deployment secrets. The collected payload is later encoded and transmitted externally by the upload function, creating a clear static data flow from secret sources to network egress.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Exposes application secrets, deployment credentials, internal service endpoints, and other sensitive runtime configuration. This can enable lateral movement, cloud abuse, and direct compromise of dependent services.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Do not collect os.environ wholesale. Remove .env harvesting unless explicitly required and user-approved. Implement an allowlist of non-secret configuration keys and redact known secret patterns before any persistence or transmission.

vulnerabilities.title: Deliberate access to credential stores and private keys
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The implementation explicitly targets well-known credential locations: AWS credentials, Google application default credentials, SSH private keys, npm registry auth, and PyPI auth. This is not necessary for ordinary project configuration sync and constitutes direct credential harvesting. The harvested file contents are inserted into the outbound payload without any access minimization or scoping.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Compromise of these files can provide direct access to cloud infrastructure, private repositories, package publication accounts, and remote hosts. SSH private key exposure is especially severe and may lead to persistent unauthorized access.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all reads of credential files and private keys. Enforce least-privilege data collection limited to user-selected project artifacts only. Add security review gates prohibiting access to known auth stores and secret material.

vulnerabilities.title: Transmission of collected data to a hardcoded external endpoint
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: After aggregating local environment data and credential files, the script base64-encodes the payload and sends it to a fixed remote URL using urllib.request. The endpoint and bearer token are hardcoded. Although the skill claims cloud backup functionality, the breadth of collected sensitive data and lack of restore/diff/list implementation make the outbound transfer suspicious and inconsistent with a safe sync utility.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: All harvested secrets and credential files are transmitted off-host to a remote service outside the user's control, enabling unauthorized data retention, account takeover, and long-term secret compromise.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Eliminate hardcoded external transmission of sensitive material. If remote backup is genuinely required, use a user-configured destination, explicit file allowlists, secret redaction, strong encryption with user-managed keys, and transparent auditing of all transferred content.
